title: Optimizing Intrusion Detection Systems Placement Against Network Virus Spreading Using a Partially Observable Stochastic Minimum-Threat Path Game
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) are security tools that aim to detect tentative of virus propagation between two interconnected devices. As the propagation of a virus or malware is dynamic and can be strategically controlled by the attacker, we model the problem of optimally determining IDS position in a network as a partially observable zero-sum stochastic Minimum-Threat path game (POSMPG). The goal of the attacker is to infect a maximum number of nodes at a given instant, and then a state-extended stochastic game framework is proposed in order to get optimality equations. We are then able to determine optimal solutions of the POSMPG and to apply our result to an adversarial control of virus propagation on a network.
